2605.18613.title: SAME: A Semantically-Aligned Music Autoencoder
2605.18613.paper_summary: Latent representations are at the heart of the majority of modern generative models. In the audio domain they are typically produced by a neural-audio-codec autoencoder. In this work we introduce SAME (Semantically-Aligned Music autoEncoder), an autoencoder for stereo music and general audio that reaches a 4096$\times$ temporal compression ratio while maintaining reconstruction quality and downstream generative performance. We achieve this by combining a tranformer-based backbone with set of semantic regularisation approaches, phase-aware reconstruction losses and improved discriminator designs. The architecture delivers substantial computational cost benefits, through both its high compression ratio and its reliance on well-optimised transformer primitives. Two variants (a large SAME-L and a CPU-deployable SAME-S) are released in open-weights form.
2605.18613.paper_summary_zh: 潛在表示是大多數現代生成模型的核心。在音頻領域，它們通常由神經音頻編解碼器自編碼器產生。在這項工作中，我們介紹了SAME（語義對齊音樂自編碼器），這是一種用於立體音樂和一般音頻的自編碼器，達到了4096$\times$的時間壓縮比，同時保持重建質量和下游生成性能。我們通過結合基於Transformer的骨幹、語義正則化方法、相位感知重建損失和改進的鑑別器設計來實現這一點。該架構通過其高壓縮比和對優化良好的Transformer原語的依賴，提供了顯著的計算成本效益。兩個變體（大型SAME-L和可在CPU上部署的SAME-S）以開放權重形式發布。
2605.18613.author: Julian D. Parker et.al.
2605.18613.authors: Julian D. Parker, Zach Evans, CJ Carr, Zachary Zukowski, Josiah Taylor, Matthew Rice, Jordi Pons
